article_meta.title: Iranians block a street during a protest in Tehran, Iran on 9 January 2026

reasoning: The summary inherits some of the source’s loaded framing (e.g., calling the crackdown “brutal” in the model’s voice and using “anti-regime”), though it attributes “campaign of revenge” to human rights groups. It also omits the Iranian judiciary’s perspective present in the article, creating slant and omission. Most factual claims align with the article, but some causal framing (arrests for providing care) lacks explicit attribution.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses loaded terms like “brutal crackdown” and “anti-regime demonstrations” in its own voice; otherwise mostly descriptive.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States arrests are for treating protesters and implies causality without explicit attribution, though supported by rights groups in the article.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: “Brutal” is an evaluative descriptor used as fact; other evaluative phrases are attributed.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Heightened tone mainly from “brutal,” but most language remains factual; potential execution is factual context.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about internal motives beyond attributed statements.
custom_reasoning.slant: Summarizes rights groups and US position but omits the judiciary chief’s stance present in the article.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Leaves out Iranian authorities’ justification and quotes; focuses solely on critics’ accounts and US statement.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Includes “brutal” as a subjective adjective in the model’s own voice.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically charged phrasing (“anti-regime,” “crackdown”) where neutral alternatives exist.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Emphasizes deaths, arrests, and executions without balancing with the authorities’ stated rationale.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Slight deference to official/NGO sources (US State Dept, rights orgs) but not extreme.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than the most charged parts of the source but still carries loaded terms and lacks balance.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Does not include the Iranian judiciary/authorities’ perspective, which appears in the source; sources_represented is also incorrect.
